value.found_quote: Claude Code on the web runs each session in an isolated, Anthropic-managed virtual machine. A network proxy enforces a default allowlist, and a separate proxy holds your GitHub token outside the sandbox while issuing scoped credentials for repository access inside it.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes "Claude Code on the web" as a product offered by Anthropic that runs each session in an isolated, Anthropic-managed (i.e., fully managed) virtual machine. It also confirms that sessions clone repositories from GitHub and interact with GitHub (pushing changes back via scoped credentials), which aligns with the claim. The page lists it as an available option in its comparison table with "None" setup effort, requiring only "a Claude subscription and GitHub," indicating it is an active, generally available offering. The claim's description of "fully managed, isolated virtual machine, cloning repos and pushing changes back to a branch" is substantiated by the page's description of Anthropic-managed VMs, GitHub repo cloning, and the GitHub token/scoped credentials mechanism for repository access.
